name: The healing of the lunatick child
notes: All three synoptics tell of the boy with the unclean spirit, healed when Jesus comes down after the transfiguration to find the disciples unable to cast it out. The shared core is the father's plea for his son, the spirit's tearing and foaming, the disciples' failure, Christ's rebuke of a faithless generation, the rebuking of the spirit and the child's deliverance, and the private question why the disciples could not. Each evangelist keeps a complementary vantage: Matthew names the affliction 'lunatick' and gives the saying on faith as a grain of mustard seed; Mark fills in the scribes' questioning, the dumb spirit, the father's 'Lord, I believe; help thou mine unbelief,' and the boy left as one dead and raised by the hand; Luke marks the next day, the only child, and the child delivered again to his father. The endings on prayer and fasting and on unbelief stand side by side and the fuller detail in Mark and Luke is left as a matter for further study.
